What kind of country does the poet dream of?A rich countryA powerful country  A free and fearless countryA modern countryWhat does “head is held high” suggest?Pride and dignity 	(b) Anger 	(c) Carelessness 	(d) LazinessWhat does the poet want for his countrymen?Wealth 	(b) Power 	(c) Freedom and unity 	(d) ComfortThe poet wants the mind to be without ______.anger 	(b) fear 	(c) doubt 	(d) prideB. Explain the given lines from the poem with reference to  the context. Where the world has not been broken up into fragmentsBy narrow domestic walls.Where tireless striving stretches its arms towards perfection.Where the mind is led forward by theeInto ever-widening thought and action.Where the mind is without fear and the head is held high.C. Answer the questions given below. What is the theme of the poem?What does the poet talk about, ‘in particular’ in the poem?Why has the world been said to be divided by domestic walls?What are the dead habits of people?When we say ‘reason’, what does it mean?What is the poet’s appeal to God?Provide some examples of alliteration and metaphor from the poem.	Put on Your Thinking Caps	 	/Discuss in class:How can we be ‘free’ in the real sense? Quote some examples from real life.Freedom means to enjoy one’s rights. But with rights comes duty. Explain with suitable examples.Write some habits that hamper the development and success of a nation. How should people behave and act?	Dictionary & Vocabulary Skills	  	/Rabindranath Tagore chose his words very carefully. Use a dictionary to compare the meanings of the words below. Then tick the word that best completes each idea in the poem. 	Guide Words: chemistry – chlorineIdeaWord ChoicesAbsence of fear/ brave / fearless / boldAbility to think clearly/ reason / opinion / guessLove of truth	 fact 	 truth / informationFreedom of thought/ liberty / freedom / permissionRearrange the letters to form meaningful words and then make sentences for them. 	1. G I D N I T Y →  ___________________ 	 _____________________________________________________________________  	2. R E F E M D O  →  ___________________ 	 _____________________________________________________________________  	3. R U H T T →  ___________________ 	 _____________________________________________________________________  	4. O K W E G E L N D →  ___________________ 	 _____________________________________________________________________ConnectCommunicate	Speaking Skills	 	CG:2, C:2.2	Critical ThinkingThe poet gives a message of unity and harmony in the poem. What are your views on it? Speak about the idea in class. Include the given points:meaning of harmonyimportance of harmonyways to create harmonyexamples of unity	Writing Skills	  	CG:2, C:2.2	Critical ThinkingComplete the summary of the poem by filling the blanks.The poet _________________  his home country where _________________. He said no one in his country should ______________. Everyone should be __________________. All students must get _________________ without any difference _________________. India and its people should ______________. They must raise their voice _________________. The poet encourages everyone to _______________. People need to think ____________ and work towards ______________. . He then says just like a water stream that can easily ______________, we should also avoid _______________ On the in order to not get lost ________________. It means that people of his country should believe in _____________. and think with _________________.	English-7	69CompetenciesBeyondCG:2, C:2.2 Interconnected Learning with Science   Critical Thinking Read the quote given here.“Our march to freedom is irreversible.We must not allow fear to stand in our way.”	 	—Nelson MandelaExplain the quote in your own words.Now answer the questions given below.What is freedom according to you?What words come to your mind when you think of the word ‘freedom’?What different types of freedom can you think of? SEL Corner  Healthy Relationships CG:2, C:2.2 Critical Thinking ῀Read the situations given below. For each, decide what a responsible and fearless person would do. Justify your answer in 2–3 lines.Your friend is spreading a rumour about another student.You see someone being excluded because of their language or background.You are afraid to speak your opinion in class even though you know the answer is correct.A group is making fun of someone online.	 Explore 	CG:2, C:2.2	Critical ThinkingRefer to a political map of India and the world to answer the following questions.Locate India on a world map. Name the continent it belongs to.Mark India’s neighbouring countries on the map.Identify and label the states and union territories of India.Locate New Delhi on the map. Why is it important for the country?Name and locate the following on the map: 	 The Himalayas,     The Ganga River,     The Thar Desert,     The Deccan Plateau  	6. Locate countries that gained independence after colonial rule, similar to India.Scan & LearnScan the QR code to access Ask questions, clear yout the worksheet for this doubts, and learn more with chapter.AI.
